content: I, Sunita Roy, daughter of the late Shri Anil Chatterjee, residing at Village Kharagpur, P.O. Kharagpur, Dist. Paschim Medinipur, West Bengal, hereby file this FIR regarding fraudulent acquisition of ancestral agricultural land. My father, Shri Anil Chatterjee, passed away on 12/06/2018. He was the sole owner of a 2.3-acre agricultural plot in Kharagpur, which I have managed since 2005 alongside him. In 2020, my sister, Priya Chatterjee, filed a mutation application in the Kharagpur Tehsil Office claiming sole ownership based on a 2017 will. I was unaware of this will until I received notice of the mutation. Upon investigation, I discovered that the 2017 will is forged: the signature is traced, the paper is from a 2015 batch found in my father’s study, and the notary who certified it has vanished. Further, the 2016 land revenue record was digitally altered on 04/04/2019 — after my father’s death — by Revenue Clerk Debashish Mukherjee, whom I believe was bribed. A neighbor, Mrs. Anjali Ghosh, falsely testified she witnessed the signing of the will on 12/03/2017, but hospital records prove she was hospitalized from 08/03/2017 to 20/03/2017. The original revenue officer, Mr. Ramesh Das, who reviewed the mutation, was found dead in 2021 — ruled suicide, but signs of poisoning were present. Boundary stones were moved in January 2023 to claim an additional 0.4 acres, confirmed by satellite imagery from 2020. I request immediate investigation into forgery, criminal breach of trust, corruption, and possible murder. I also request protection, as I have received threats from Priya’s associates.

My father, Shri Anil Chatterjee, was in poor health since 2016. On 12/03/2017, he called me, my sister Sunita, and our neighbor Mrs. Anjali Ghosh to his residence. He declared he wished to leave the entire property to me, as Sunita had shown no interest in farming and I had taken care of his medical expenses. He signed the will in front of us all. The document was notarized the same day by Notary P. K. Banerjee, whose seal is on the document. I withdrew ₹5 lakh from my father’s account on 11/03/2017 to pay legal fees for drafting the will. The land revenue record was updated in 2016 because my father had already decided to make me the sole heir. I never had any motive to forge anything. I was deeply attached to my father and cared for him until his death. I have no idea why Sunita is accusing me. As for the boundary stones — they were placed according to the original survey. The satellite images she refers to are outdated. I did not bribe anyone. I am the rightful heir.

I lived with my father from 2005 until his death in 2018. He always said he would divide the land equally between my sister and me. He never mentioned a will. In early 2017, he told me he was drafting a note with Dr. Arjun Mehta as trustee to ensure fairness. I found that handwritten note in his drawer after his death — it was on my own notebook, which I bought in January 2015. I never saw any signed will. When Priya filed the mutation in 2020, I was shocked. I checked the revenue records — the 2016 entry showing me as non-heir was clearly altered. The notary’s seal is fake; the firm closed in 2016. Mrs. Ghosh couldn’t have witnessed the signing — she was in the hospital. The ₹5 lakh withdrawal was not for legal fees — it went to a shell company Priya created. I have bank statements. The boundary stones were moved in January 2023 — I have photos. Mr. Ramesh Das, the officer who reviewed the mutation, was found dead with a note in his pocket that said, 'They know I know.' I believe he was poisoned. Priya is in debt. She was embezzling from my father’s savings. She hired Nandini Sen to type the will — but Nandini was on leave that week. I have her employer’s records. Priya is a criminal.

I served as Revenue Clerk from 2010 to 2020. The 2016 land revenue record was updated in 2016 as per the request of Shri Anil Chatterjee. He submitted a handwritten note stating he wished to transfer the entire property to his daughter Priya. I verified the document and updated the record accordingly. I have no knowledge of any forgery. The digital records were maintained properly. I retired in 2020. I do not know why anyone would accuse me. I have no connection to any bribes. I am a man of integrity. I was never threatened. I have no idea about the death of Mr. Ramesh Das.

metadata.relation_to_case: Former Typist, Claimed to Have Typed the Will
metadata.occupation: Typist (Currently Absent, Reported to Have Fled to Nepal)

content: I worked at Shree Vani Typing Center from 2014 to 2021. I was hired by a woman named Priya in March 2017 to type a document for her father. I typed a single-page will on 10/03/2017. I did not read its contents. She gave me a handwritten draft and asked me to make it formal. I signed an affidavit to that effect. I was not paid much. I do not know if the signature was real. I was on leave from 08/03/2017 to 14/03/2017 due to my mother’s illness — I submitted a leave form. But I did go to work on 10/03/2017 because my sister was hospitalized and I had to deliver the document. I don’t know why my attendance log says I was absent. I have no idea about the notary or the land records. I left India because I feared for my life after receiving a call from an unknown number saying, 'You’re the only loose end.'

metadata.relation_to_case: Distant Cousin, Claimed Trustee in 2015 Note

content: I am a distant cousin of the late Anil Chatterjee. In 2015, he gave me a handwritten note on a notebook page, asking me to act as trustee to ensure his daughters received equal shares of the land. He said he was drafting a formal will but wanted a safekeeping. I kept it in my personal files. I never signed it. I never acted on it. I have no knowledge of the 2017 will. I never received any communication from Priya about the property. I do not know why Sunita says the notebook belongs to her — I have the original page. I only saw the note once. I have no financial interest in this land. I am merely a custodian of a family document.

I knew Anil Chatterjee for over 30 years. He was a kind man. On the morning of 12/03/2017, I was at his house. He called me in to witness him sign a will leaving the land to Priya. I saw him sign it. I signed as a witness. I remember the day because it was a sunny Tuesday, and I had just finished my morning walk. I have no reason to lie. I was not in the hospital then. I don’t know why my diary says otherwise — I must have written it wrong. I am 72 years old. I cannot remember every date. But I know what I saw.

This is a reconstructed statement from the personal diary of Ramesh Das, recovered from his residence after his death on 18/07/2021. Entry dated 15/04/2020: 'Mutation application filed by Priya Chatterjee on forged will. Paper from 2015 batch. Ink from Parker pen bought in March 2017. Signature traced. Notary seal is fake — firm closed in 2016. Debashish Mukherjee altered 2016 record — digital trace shows edit on 04/04/2019. Nandini Sen was on leave. Mrs. Ghosh hospitalized. Bank withdrawal to shell company. This is fraud. I will report to CBI tomorrow. If anything happens to me — it’s Priya.'

Examination of the 2017 Will (E1): 1. Paper: Spectral analysis confirms the paper is from a batch manufactured in January 2015, consistent with stationery found in Anil Chatterjee’s study. 2. Ink: Chromatography matches Parker Pen Model 51, purchased by Priya Chatterjee on 05/03/2017 from a local stationery shop in Kharagpur (receipt attached). 3. Signature: Digital enhancement reveals tracing patterns consistent with use of a lightbox — the signature is a trace of a prior genuine signature, not an original. 4. Notary Seal: The seal impression matches a stolen stamp from 'Roy & Associates,' a law firm that ceased operations in 2016. The notary's signature is a photocopied forgery. Conclusion: The 2017 will is a forged document, created after 05/03/2017 using paper from 2015 and ink from a pen purchased by Priya Chatterjee.

metadata.issued_by: National Cyber Forensics Unit, New Delhi

content: Examination of the Kharagpur Tehsil Digital Land Record (2016 Entry): 1. Metadata of the digital file shows the last edit occurred on 04/04/2019 at 14:23 IST. 2. IP address traced to a terminal in the Tehsil Office, logged in with Debashish Mukherjee’s credentials. 3. The original paper copy was reported destroyed in a 2019 fire — but the digital file was not backed up until after the fire, indicating premeditated deletion. 4. No authorized access log exists for the date of alteration. Conclusion: The 2016 land revenue record was digitally altered posthumously by Debashish Mukherjee to support Priya Chatterjee’s mutation claim.

metadata.issued_by: State Bank of India, Kharagpur Branch

content: Transaction History of Account No. SBIN/123456789 (Anil Chatterjee): - 11/03/2017: ₹5,00,000 withdrawn as cash transfer to 'P. C. Enterprises' (Account No. SBIN/987654321). Transaction Code: 'CT-UNREG' (Unregistered Entity). - 15/03/2017: ₹12,00,000 transferred from Anil’s account to Priya Chatterjee’s personal account. - 12/08/2021: ₹8,50,000 transferred from Priya’s account to Dubai-based account 'P. Chatterjee' (SWIFT: DUBAI123). The shell company 'P. C. Enterprises' was registered on 02/06/2016 with a fake PAN. Priya Chatterjee is the sole director. The ₹5 lakh withdrawal was not for legal fees — no legal invoice or receipt exists. The transfer to Dubai matches Priya’s passport details.

metadata.issued_by: Kharagpur Tehsil Office, Land Records Division

content: Land Record No. KHR/AGRI/2018/0072: Plot No. 12B, Village Kharagpur, 2.3 acres. Original owner: Anil Chatterjee. Mutation Application (2020): Filed by Priya Chatterjee, claiming sole inheritance under 2017 will. Original 2016 record (paper copy) was destroyed in fire on 15/02/2019. Digital record updated on 04/04/2019 to reflect Priya as sole heir. Survey Map submitted by Priya (2020) shows boundary extending 15m east. However, satellite imagery from 2018 (Google Earth Archive) shows no such extension. Boundary stone at eastern edge installed on 15/01/2023, as per contractor’s invoice (attached). No official survey was filed in 2020. The 2020 survey coordinates match the 2018 survey — indicating fabrication.

Autopsy Report: Deceased: Ramesh Das, Male, 58. Cause of Death: Cardiac arrest. Toxicology Report: Presence of lethal dose of curare alkaloid (natural poison) in bloodstream. No prior medical history of cardiac condition. No signs of struggle. Note found in pocket: 'They know I know.' Death ruled suicide by local police, but forensic evidence indicates poisoning. Time of death: Between 22:00 and 00:00 on 17/07/2021. No fingerprints on poison container. Poison likely administered via food or drink. No suspects identified yet. Case referred to CBI.

WhatsApp Message Chain: Priya Chatterjee (9876543210) to Dr. Arjun Mehta (9988776655), dated 14/06/2016: 'You’re the only one who can keep this safe — don’t let Sunita get her hands on it.' Attached: Image of the 2015 handwritten note. Message deleted from Priya’s phone but recovered from cloud backup. Dr. Mehta claims he received it as a 'courtesy' — but the message shows intent to conceal the note from Sunita. No prior communication between them on property matters before this date. Message sent from Priya’s home IP address.

CCTV footage from Anil Chatterjee’s residence (2017): No cameras were installed inside the house. External camera at gate shows Priya entering on 12/03/2017 at 10:15 AM. Mrs. Anjali Ghosh does not appear on camera that day. Footage shows Mrs. Ghosh entering her own house at 10:30 AM on 15/03/2017 — confirming she was hospitalized on 12/03/2017. Boundary stone installation on 15/01/2023: Footage from nearby shop shows Priya’s hired contractor, Raju Singh, moving two stones with a tractor at 8:00 AM. Priya is visible at the site at 8:45 AM, handing him cash. No official surveyor present.

Investigation Summary: This case involves a complex property fraud centered on forgery, corruption, and possible homicide. Forensic evidence confirms the 2017 will is forged. The 2016 land record was altered by Debashish Mukherjee. Nandini Sen was on leave when the will was typed. Mrs. Ghosh was hospitalized and could not have witnessed the signing. Dr. Mehta’s involvement is passive — he was used to lend credibility. Financial trail shows Priya Chatterjee embezzled ₹12 lakh from her father’s account and transferred ₹8.5 lakh to Dubai. The death of Ramesh Das was homicide by curare poisoning. Boundary stones were illegally moved by Priya’s contractor. Evidence points to Priya Chatterjee as the principal architect of the fraud. Accomplices: Debashish Mukherjee (bribery), Nandini Sen (forged affidavit), and possibly Nandini Sen’s employer who falsified attendance records. Motive: Debt and desire to sell land to real estate developer for ₹4.2 crore. Recommendation: Charge sheet under IPC Sections 420 (cheating), 467 (forgery of valuable security), 468 (forgery for purpose of cheating), 302 (murder), 120B (criminal conspiracy), and Prevention of Corruption Act. Arrest warrants issued for Priya Chatterjee, Debashish Mukherjee. Nandini Sen is absconding. Dr. Mehta and Mrs. Ghosh are not criminally liable but their testimonies are unreliable.

IN THE COURT OF THE SESSIONS JUDGE, PASCHIM MEDINIPUR

CHARGE SHEET UNDER SECTION 173 CrPC

Accused: 1. Priya Chatterjee, 2. Debashish Mukherjee, 3. Nandini Sen (absconding)

Offences: 1. IPC 420 - Cheating 2. IPC 467 - Forgery of Valuable Security 3. IPC 468 - Forgery for Purpose of Cheating 4. IPC 120B - Criminal Conspiracy 5. IPC 302 - Murder (of Ramesh Das) 6. Prevention of Corruption Act, 1988 - Section 7 (taking gratification)

Facts: The accused, Priya Chatterjee, forged a will dated 12/03/2017, using a Parker pen purchased by her on 05/03/2017 and paper from her father’s 2015 stationery. The signature was traced. She bribed Debashish Mukherjee to alter the 2016 land record on 04/04/2019. She hired Nandini Sen to type the will while she was on leave, and obtained a false affidavit. She transferred ₹12 lakh from her father’s account to her own and ₹8.5 lakh to Dubai. She moved boundary stones illegally. Ramesh Das, who discovered the fraud, was poisoned. Evidence: Forensic report, digital forensics, financial records, CCTV, WhatsApp message, autopsy report, hospital records, attendance logs. The accused are jointly liable for criminal conspiracy and murder.

JUDGE: The court has reviewed the forensic reports, digital evidence, and witness testimonies. The will is forged. The land record altered. The witness testimony of Mrs. Ghosh is discredited. The death of Ramesh Das is under murder investigation. The accused, Priya Chatterjee, is present. The prosecution has submitted the charge sheet. Defense counsel, Mr. Sen Gupta, argues that no direct evidence ties Priya to the poisoning. The court notes: the forged documents, the financial trail, the boundary tampering, and the motive are overwhelming. The defense has not produced any credible evidence to refute the forensic findings. The court declares Priya Chatterjee as the principal offender. Debashish Mukherjee is charged with corruption. Nandini Sen is declared absconding. The court orders arrest warrants for all accused. Hearing adjourned until 05/09/2023 for framing of charges.
